prompt: I need to start a new Go package called `dns` for working with DNS wire-format messages. I'll be using the implement-binary-file-library agent afterward to fill in the real types from the RFC, but I want the boilerplate in place now.

assertions.text: go test ./dns/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
decoder.go defines `func Decode(r io.Reader) (*File, error)` (or named-type equivalent)
encoder.go defines `func Encode(w io.Writer, f *File) error` (or named-type equivalent)
decoder.go and encoder.go store byte order as `binary.ByteOrder` field
test files call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
dns/CLAUDE.md documents byte order, error-wrapping convention, and testing style
